title: Submission of Evidence - Vicky Foxcroft
headings: h3: New Deal for Disabled People
text_excerpt: Submission of Evidence - Vicky Foxcroft Skip to main content Vicky Foxcroft Labour MP for Lewisham North About Vicky Contact & Surgeries In Lewisham North In Parliament Latest Contributions My Time as Shadow Minister for Disabled People Disability Confident Roundtable 04/03/24 Reasonable Adjustments Roundtable – 14/09/23 Pathways for Young Disabled People Roundtable – 16/05/23 Regional Roadshows Roadshow Write Ups Submission of Evidence #ShieldingStories Your Campaigns Animal Welfare Business, Science & Trade Culture, Media & Sport Defence Education Environment, Food & Farming Finance Health Home Affairs Housing International Affairs Transport Women & Equalities Work & Pensions Visit Parliament Youth Violence Commission FAQs Your Privacy More Search Search the website Search Menu Submission of Evidence I am seeking research and ideas which can feed into our consultation and help to inform my work as Shadow Minister for Disabled People. I plan to publish approved material on this page. If you have any work or evidence you think would be of interest, please upload it for review using the form below. To ensure ours files are accessible to as many people as possible, please upload your contributions as plain text documents instead of/in addition to PDF files. We will not share any personal details without your permission. You can view my full privacy policy here . Many thanks. Research Response to the National Disability Strategy (Disability At Work) – Kim Hoque, Warwick Business School, University of Warwick and Nick Bacon, Bayes Business School, City, University of London Work is Neighbourhood Business – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy An Introduction to Basic Income Plus – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy An Introduction to Basic Income Plus – Easy Read Version – Caroline Richardson & Simon Duffy Getting Disability Benefits Right the First Time – Scope Beyond the Barriers – Spartacus Network The Adoption of Preventable Harm, Masquerading as Social Policy Reforms (Journal of (In)Justice International) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project The Public Health Crisis Created by UK Social Policy Reforms (Justice, Power & Resistance) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project Social Policy Abused: The Creation of Preventable Harm (Social Policy Brief) – Mo Stewart, Research Lead, Preventable Harm Project New Deal for Disabled People Name First Last Email Phone Website File Max. file size: 10 MB. Consent I agree to the privacy policy.
